label: Shutterhouse Sleep Niche
description: Shutterhouse Sleep Niche is a compact bedroom carved from fused basalt within Terminus Shutterhouse, laid out as a recessed chamber off the residence’s narrow axial hall. A low, saltstone-lined sleeping alcove is set into the inner wall, while deep-cut niches in the basalt serve as built-in storage to minimize loose furnishings under persistent tidal stresses. Layered interior shutters and glare-baffles seal a deep-set aperture, admitting controlled terminator light while excluding mineral-laden haze. Thick partition walls hold a stable, cool temperature, and the floor is finished with polished saltstone that resists salt dust accumulation.
